title: Those damn Chins
wikitext:
[]: Those damn Chins Post by wonderluster » Mon Jul 27, 2009 9:19 pm Lock me away if it seems reasonable Reichu, but I must ask, why do all Evas in exception of the MP have some sort of large chin? I mean in the Manga we got a good glimpse at Evas face, and Relvotech figures show the face as well but they all show a more human face with no reason for and abnormally large CHIN. This bothers me.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 27, 2009 9:25 pm Here's (possibly) a better way of phrasing this question: do depictions of Oni (which the Evangelion are based off of) have prominent/pointy chins?

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 27, 2009 9:56 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: do depictions of Oni (which the Evangelion are based off of) have prominent/pointy chins?

[]: Post by wonderluster » Mon Jul 27, 2009 10:02 pm What so would that mean that the Pylons could be part of the Oni depiction as well?

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 27, 2009 10:46 pm Mecha design aesthetic demands as many wacky doodads sticking out as possible. "I hate to say it, but Ikuto-kun somehow managed not to put enough wacky doodads on Eva-02." "Put nipples on the chin. That will help emphasize the production's maternal themes." "Great idea, Anno-san!"

[]: Post by wonderluster » Mon Jul 27, 2009 11:04 pm Well that explains a lot of the Gundam universe

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jul 28, 2009 8:56 am For the same reason the Eva 01 has a useless giant horn on his head. It just looks cool. I believe NERV and Seele wanted to disguise the Evas as robots so the Japanese government don't get what's their up to. To have external armor that is completely form-fitting would kinda give away the fact that the Evas are synthetic life forms, not robots.

[]: Post by wonderluster » Tue Jul 28, 2009 9:43 am Thanks for the help!

[]: Re: Those damn Chins Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jul 28, 2009 9:46 am wonderluster wrote: Lock me away if it seems reasonable Reichu, but I must ask, why do all Evas in exception of the MP have some sort of large chin?

[]: Post by Shinji-kun » Tue Jul 28, 2009 10:09 am It's not even close to gendo's nose.

[]: Post by cambaprecoz » Tue Jul 28, 2009 3:27 pm Shinji-kun wrote: It's not even close to gendo's nose.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jul 28, 2009 4:53 pm Don't make fun of Gendo's nose, hes very sensitive about that. I agree, unit 02 does "seem" to have a big chin but everyone is built differently.

[]: Post by Ramiel² » Tue Jul 28, 2009 5:44 pm Shinji-kun wrote: It's not even close to gendo's nose.

[]: Post by Shinji-kun » Tue Jul 28, 2009 6:00 pm He's such an overlord.

[]: Post by thewayneiac » Tue Jul 28, 2009 6:10 pm Shinji-kun wrote: It's not even close to gendo's nose.

[]: Post by wonderluster » Tue Jul 28, 2009 9:46 pm That seemed somewhat pleasant...

[]: Post by schismatics » Tue Jul 28, 2009 9:52 pm Gendo wrote: "My nose is Gargantuan! You little Pig-snout, you tiny Monkey-Nostrils, you virtually invisible Pekinese-Puss, don't you realize that a nose like mine is both scepter and orb, a monument to me superiority? A great nose is the banner of a great man, a generous heart, a towering spirit, an expansive soul--such as I unmistakably am, and such as you dare not to dream of being, with your bilious weasel's eyes and no nose to keep them apart! With your face as lacking in all distinction--as lacking, I say, in interest, as lacking in pride, in imagination, in honesty, in lyricism--in a word, as lacking in nose as that other offensively bland expanse at the opposite end of your cringing spine--which I now remove from my sight by stringent application of my boot!"
